title: euphorbia grandicornis subsp. grandicornis
intro: Euphorbia grandicornis subsp. grandicornis, also known as the Devil's Backbone or Pincushion Plant, is a succulent plant species native to South Africa. It is traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating digestive issues. In herbal preparations, Euphorbia grandicornis subsp. grandicornis can be used to make infusions, decoctions, or ointments, often in combination with other plants, to harness its medicinal properties.
